title: Capability-aware terminal color rendering with NO_COLOR support
source: Established terminal UX standards: no-color.org spec, CLIG (clig.dev) guidelines, FORCE_COLOR/NO_COLOR conventions (knowledge through Jan 2026)
description: The Rick Terminal output should degrade gracefully across truecolor (24-bit), 256-color, 16-color, and no-color environments. Many CI logs, piped output, and accessibility tools strip or choke on raw ANSI. Honoring NO_COLOR and detecting TTY/COLORTERM makes Rick's output readable everywhere and accessible to color-blind users, without losing the themed flair when a capable terminal is present.
proposed_changes: Add a Capabilities detector in visual.py that reads NO_COLOR, FORCE_COLOR, COLORTERM, TERM and sys.stdout.isatty() once at import. Route all color emission through a single colorize(text, fg, style) helper that picks truecolor escape codes, falls back to the nearest 256/16-color code, or returns plain text when color is disabled or output is not a TTY. Replace any inline hardcoded ANSI sequences with calls to this helper so piped/CI output is clean.
